race.name: La Stelvio Santini
race.tagline: Alpine legend: conquer the Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio in Italy's most celebrated granfondo

climate.description: Alpine June conditions with variable mountain weather. Expect crisp air and sunshine, but prepare for rapid weather changes including potential snow and cold temperatures at elevation.

features: High Alpine terrain featuring two of Europe's most iconic climbs: the Passo dello Stelvio and Mortirolo
Routes traverse spectacular mountain scenery with steep gradients, technical descents, and sustained elevation gain

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 80. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 77.

course_description.character: Epic Alpine granfondo featuring two of cycling's most iconic and historically significant climbs. The event combines sustained elevation gain, technical descents, and high-altitude mountain passages that echo the routes of Giro d'Italia legends. All routes start and finish in Bormio town center.
course_description.signature_challenge: The combined Mortirolo-Stelvio double on the long route, replicating one of the Giro d'Italia's most brutal mountain stages. Riders face back-to-back legendary climbs with minimal recovery, testing both physical capacity and mental resilience.
suffering_zones: Mortirolo ascent: relentless gradient and altitude stress on medium and long routes
Passo dello Stelvio final climb: sustained steep sections with thin air and exposed terrain
Teglio approach: grinding mid-ride elevation gain before major climbs
High-altitude descents: technical and exposed, demanding focus and bike handling

logistics.airport: MXP (Milan Malpensa), 200km, 3.5hr drive; BGY (Bergamo Orio al Serio), 180km, 3hr drive; INN (Innsbruck (Austria)), 150km, 2.5hr drive
logistics.lodging_strategy: Bormio offers range from budget to luxury options. Bikehotel Funivia and QC Terme are mentioned as partner accommodations with cycling-specific amenities including bike storage, repair facilities, and massage services. Book early as event attracts 3,500 riders.
logistics.transport: Event based in Bormio, a well-established Alpine cycling destination in northern Italy. Start and finish in town center. Multiple accommodation and support options available. Nearest major airports require 2-4 hour transfers.

history.origin_story: La Stelvio Santini (formerly Granfondo Stelvio Santini) is one of Italy's most celebrated granfondo events, rebranded in 2024. The event has grown to rank among the top 10 long-distance cycling events worldwide, attracting increasing numbers of international participants annually. The granfondo showcases the legendary Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio climbs, which gained iconic status through Giro d'Italia history, particularly the brutal 2012 stage featuring both ascents.
notable_moments: Rebranding to La Stelvio Santini in 2024 with route modifications due to roadworks
Introduction of gravel route variant in 2026, expanding event diversity
Consistent growth to 3,000-3,500 annual participants from across Europe and beyond
Recognition as top-10 global granfondo, cementing prestige among international cycling community
Couple registration category with special finish-line prizes, adding inclusive social dimension

biased_opinion.summary: La Stelvio Santini represents the pinnacle of Alpine granfondo racing. The dual ascent of Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio in a single day is a bucket-list experience for serious cyclists, offering unmatched climbing pedigree and mountain scenery. The event's three-route structure democratizes participation while maintaining elite challenge. Logistics are solid, though the remote location and June timing require careful planning. For cyclists seeking legendary terrain and international community, this is essential.

final_verdict.one_liner: La Stelvio Santini is an elite Alpine granfondo that delivers legendary climbing, international prestige, and bucket-list mountain scenery in a well-organized, accessible format.
final_verdict.should_you_race: La Stelvio Santini ranks among the world's premier granfondo events, justified by its dual ascent of the Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio—two of cycling's most storied climbs. The event excels in climb quality (5/5), scenery (5/5), prestige (5/5), and international appeal (5/5). Three route options democratize participation while maintaining elite challenge. Organization is professional with solid logistics, mechanical support, and feed stations. The June timing offers favorable Alpine weather, though mountain conditions remain unpredictable. Primary drawbacks are travel costs to remote Bormio location and registration fees (€90-130) that don't include accommodation. For serious cyclists seeking legendary terrain, international community, and unmatched climbing pedigree, La Stelvio Santini is essential. Tier T1 event with 88/100 overall score.

videos.title: Granfondo Stelvio Santini - An In Depth Look At Climbing The Iconic Stelvio Pass
videos.description:
The Gran Fondo Stelvio is one of the toughest sportives for any amateur cyclist and GCN takes a close look at the final climb - the Stelvio. 
Subscribe to GCN for more insight into cycling: http://gcn.eu/gcnsubs

At 21.5km this is one of the most famous climbs in European cycling with precipitous hairpin switchbacks that climb to 2790m altitude, and GCN takes a close look at the climb to give you tips and tactics. Oh, and some stunning scenery!

The Gran Fondo Stelvio is 154km long, and includes the fearsome Mortirolo as well as the mighty Stelvio, and the GCN staff will be tackling it this summer - as a special treat. 

You can enter the Grandfondo here http://www.granfondostelviosantini.com
 
Music: Basil Simon: http://gcn.eu/VWoftN

About GCN:

The Global Cycling Network puts you in the centre of the action: from the iconic summit of the Stelvio to the epic trails of Fort William, Scotland, everywhere there is pavé or dirt, world-class racing, and pro riders, we will be there bringing you all the action, essential analysis and unparalleled access every week, every month, and every year.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside cycling

Youtube Channel - http://gcn.eu/gcnYT
Facebook - http://gcn.eu/gcnFb
Google+ - http://gcn.eu/gcnGPlus
Twitter - http://gcn.eu/gcnTW

Leave us a comment below!

videos.curation_reason: High-quality course preview from GCN with detailed analysis of the iconic Stelvio Pass climb.

videos.title: Granfondo Stelvio Santini 2023 Race Day Highlights Epic Cycling Event North Italy @SantiniCycling
videos.curation_reason: Recent 2023 race day highlights showing the actual event experience.

quotes.text: At 2757 meters which marks the top of the Stelvio pass, it's that coupled with the legendary riders that have conquered this climb in the past that make this climb so iconic. When you get to this altitude over 2,500 meters, lack of oxygen really just starts to tell.

This climb in particular is going to take at least an hour and a half so you shouldn't be tempted to keep up with some of the faster riders around you. There are some dramatic changes in altitude and also some very dramatic changes in temperature.

key_challenges.name: Final 4 kilometers
key_challenges.description: After reaching the only flat rest section, riders face the hardest 4 kilometers of climbing before the top. The altitude over 2,500m makes the lack of oxygen really start to tell, combined with already riding for over an hour.

key_challenges.name: Final 1.5 kilometers
key_challenges.description: The last kilometer and a half requires gaining about 100m in altitude before reaching the crest, forcing riders into their easiest gears.

terrain_notes.text: There is only one small flat section that provides rest bite on the entire climb - riders should make the most of this brief respite.
The altitude effects become severe over 2,500m where lack of oxygen really starts to tell, regardless of gradient steepness.

gear_mentions.text: A 39/25 gear ratio is needed quite a lot on this climb, though riders suggest something even easier would be helpful.

race_day_tips.text: Pacing is crucial - consider it not only over the whole event but also on each of the climbs. Don't be tempted to keep up with faster riders around you.
Factor in dramatic changes in temperature due to the dramatic changes in altitude when selecting kit.
The climb will take at least an hour and a half, so pace accordingly for this duration.

additional_quotes.text: While you can't kick a football around Wembley or Old Trafford you are able to ride over the same climbs that you see the professionals do

rider_intel.search_text: The La Stelvio Santini Granfondo features the legendary Stelvio Pass climb reaching 2,757 meters elevation, making it one of the few European climbs over 2,000 meters. Riders report the climb takes at least 90 minutes with only one flat rest section on the entire ascent. The final 4 kilometers are described as the hardest, where altitude over 2,500m causes severe oxygen deprivation effects. The last 1.5 kilometers require gaining 100m elevation to reach the crest. Temperature changes dramatically with altitude variations. Pacing is critical - riders shouldn't try keeping up with faster competitors. Low gearing like 39/25 is essential though even easier ratios are recommended. The route follows the same roads used by professional cyclists in the Giro d'Italia, adding to its iconic status among amateur riders seeking the ultimate climbing challenge.
